_comment: Canonical inventory for deprecated/removed/to-retire terms. Update status and search_terms when migrating or removing. See AI_LEGACY_COMPATIBILITY_GUIDE.md.

active_or_candidate_inventory.exit_criteria: Portability acceptance criteria met; root .ruff.toml deprecated or documented.
active_or_candidate_inventory.notes: 2026-03-28: search_terms emptied while bridge remains — LEGACY_REFERENCE_REPORT uses inventory metadata only for this entry; grep-style hits duplicated module docstrings/CLI help and implied remediation work when none is required (V5 Phase 1). Re-add discrete terms only if a second mirror implementation must be detected. Bridge module: development_tools/config/sync_ruff_toml.py. Prior: 2026-03-26 phrase-only terms; 2026-03-27 logger migration.

removed_inventory.note: Removed unused _get_fallback_response bridge from ai/chatbot.py after search showed no active callers; current fallback path is _get_contextual_fallback.
Moved to core/time_utilities.parse_timestamp_for_normalization; no longer legacy bridge.
Zip read/restore/validate removed from backup_manager; directory-only backups.

Removed synthesis of coverage_outcome when missing; payloads without it are invalidated. 2026-03-24: removed details.coverage_outcome cache fallback in _extract_cached_main_coverage_state; state-only track payloads (missing classification) invalidate run_test_coverage_results.json.

Removed read fallbacks: flat development_tools/<domain>/jsons/*_results.json and dot-caches; unscoped analysis_detailed_results.json and reports/jsons/tool_timings.json loads in orchestration/timings. Loaders use jsons/scopes/** and reports/scopes/** only (V5 §7.16). legacy_flat_jsons_dir() retained for cleanup; --clear-cache still deletes orphan unscoped files.
Historical scripts/flaky_detector.py path retired after migration to tracked development_tools/tests/flaky_detector.py and CLI command flaky-detector (V5 §3.12).
Historical scripts/testing/verify_process_cleanup.py path retired after migration to tracked development_tools/tests/verify_process_cleanup.py and CLI command verify-process-cleanup (V5 §3.13).
Canonical cleanup owner remains development_tools/shared/fix_project_cleanup.py; historical scripts/cleanup_project.py path treated as retired for current tracked tree.
Root .bandit (INI) removed; Bandit config lives in pyproject.toml [tool.bandit]; analyze_bandit.py passes -c pyproject.toml. Requirements use bandit[toml].

Root pyrightconfig.json (JSONC) removed; Pyright baseline is [tool.pyright] in pyproject.toml; static_analysis.pyright_project_path default is pyproject.toml; development_tools/config/pyrightconfig.json retained for owned --project audits.

Runtime and persistence are v2-native for tasks, notebook, check-ins, templates, and deliveries; v1-shaped task merges, flat check-in answer fallbacks, message/delivery alias normalization, and flow-state task_id bridges removed or reduced to one-time key normalization on load. 2026-05-01: legacy_scan_patterns.user_data_v1_runtime_adapters removed entirely (see removed_inventory legacy_scan_patterns_user_data_v1_runtime_adapters); that bucket was heuristic noise, not v1 detection.

Notebook flow confirmations and entry references use canonical no-dash short IDs via generate_short_id / format_short_id; obsolete kind[0]-uuid6 and n-hex dashed forms removed. is_valid_entry_reference rejects dashed nlj-hex patterns.

Message editor dialog reads/writes v2 template fields only (text, schedule.days/periods, id). Removed UI fallbacks for top-level message/message_id/days/time_periods and legacy-shaped save payloads. core.message_management._message_template_to_runtime no longer falls back to message_id for id. On-disk migration and _message_template_default_to_v2 still accept legacy shapes when loading unmigrated JSON.

Removed scripts/user_data_migration.py (migrate_user_data_root, per-domain migrate_* helpers). v1→v2 transforms are no longer shipped; use backups and core.user_data_v2_envelopes.validate_v2_document. 2026-05-01: scripts/migrate_user_data_v2.py stub also deleted. 2026-05-01: redundant FORBIDDEN_V1_FIELD_NAMES_BY_DOCUMENT pre-scan removed; validate_v2_document uses Pydantic extra=forbid only.

Removed core.schemas.validate_messages_file_dict, MessageModel, and MessagesFileModel (v1 flat template shape). Message template files are validated via core.user_data_v2_envelopes / runtime v2 paths only.
scripts/migrate_sent_messages_timestamps.py deleted (was exit-1 stub from 2026-04-30). v1 sent_messages messages[] paths unsupported; v2 deliveries[] is canonical.
scripts/migrate_user_data_v2.py deleted (formerly stderr notice + exit 1 after user_data_migration removal). No in-repo user-data migration CLI.

scripts/repair_checkins_offline.py deleted. Bad checkins.json: restore from backup or hand-edit to v2 + validate_v2_document + save_json_data (USER_DATA_MODEL.md §2.7). 2026-05-01: normalize_checkins_envelope_for_repair removed from core (no shipped repair path).

Removed core.response_tracking.normalize_checkins_envelope_for_repair and _prepare_dict_for_checkin_repair_builder; tests/unit/test_normalize_checkins_repair.py deleted. Use backup, hand-edit to v2, validate_v2_document.

Removed legacy_scan_patterns.user_data_v1_runtime_adapters from this file. Patterns (.get timestamp/body, task_id literals, split task filenames, etc.) produced pervasive false positives on v2/email/dev-tools code. fix_legacy_references.REQUIRED_LEGACY_PATTERN_KEYS updated; LEGACY_REFERENCE_REPORT no longer emits a User Data V1 Runtime Adapters section from this bucket.
scripts/verify_user_data_v2.py deleted. Validate on-disk JSON via validate_v2_document in code, tests, or development_tools audits.

Renamed modules: core/user_data_v2.py -> user_data_v2_envelopes.py; user_data_manager.py -> user_data_operations.py; schedule_management.py -> schedule_runtime.py; user_data_schedule_defaults.py -> schedule_document_defaults.py. Removed core/user_data_updates.py (update_user_* merged into user_data_write.py). get_user_info_for_schedule_management -> get_user_info_for_schedule_runtime. Module-level singleton in user_data_operations remains named user_data_manager for API stability.
